title: "The person below you game"

### Post by NovaAesa on 2008-01-09
Okay, the rules of this game are quite simple. Each post makes a statement which can be either true or false. The statement has to be in reference to the person that will post next. Posts should also say if the above statement about them is true or false. Each post should be in the following format:

1) Say whether the statement in the above post is true or false.
2) Make a new statement about the person who will post next.

Okay, I will go first:

The next person to post will be a cat person rather than a dog person.

---

### Post by use a name on 2008-01-09

Cats on the other hand... They are funny (no, not lolcatz), independent and weird. They kill your furniture though.

I have neither. :)

The next person skips breakfast. :shock:

### Post by Lord Illidan on 2008-01-17
False, 
**Zaphod Beeblebrox** is a [fictional character]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fictional_character") in the various versions of the humorous [science fiction]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Science_fiction") story *[The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Hitchhiker%27s_Guide_to_the_Galaxy")* by [Douglas Adams]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Douglas_Adams") who based him on his [Cambridge]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cambridge") contemporary, Johnny Simpson.[[1]]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zaphod_Beeblebrox#_note-gaiman")
 He is from a planet in the vicinity of [Betelgeuse]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betelgeuse"), and is a "semi-cousin" of [Ford Prefect]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ford_Prefect_%28character%29"), with whom he "shares three of the same mothers". Due to "an accident with a [contraceptive]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Birth_control") and a [time machine]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Time_travel_in_fiction")", his direct ancestors from his father (Zaphod Beeblebrox the Second) are also his direct descendants (see [Zaphod Beeblebrox the Fourth]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minor_characters_in_The_Hitchhiker%27s_Guide_to_the_Galaxy#Zaphod_Beeblebrox_the_Fourth")).

### Post by crazyness003 on 2008-12-07
true. 
zfs: almost limitless filesizes (16 [EiB]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Exbibyte")) and near limitless volumesizes (2^18 [EiB]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Exbibyte") (2^78 bytes)) with 1 nanosecond timestaps
ext3: Max filesizes 16 [GiB]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gibibyte") to 2 [TiB]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tebibyte")  ,  max volumesize: 2 [TiB]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tebibyte") to 32 [TiB]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tebibyte") with 1 second timestaps

See the whole speil here [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comparison_of_file_systems](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comparison_of_file_systems)

The next person wishes there was an open standard for everything, resulting in seamless integration between systems.

>  Welcome to FreeNAS
Friday, 24 March 2006
FreeNAS is a free NAS (Network-Attached Storage) server, supporting: CIFS (samba), FTP, NFS, AFP, RSYNC, iSCSI protocols, S.M.A.R.T., local user authentication, Software RAID (0,1,5) with a Full WEB configuration interface. FreeNAS takes less than 32MB once installed on Compact Flash, hard drive or USB key.

### Post by edjones on 2009-05-28
Hi,
This is a variation of the Person Above You game. But it is different. You will post something about the person below you. The next poster will reply with True or False, and then make a true or false statement about the person that will post below them. For example:

 **[B]First Poster:**[/B] The person below me is out and proud! 



  **Second Poster:** False, I am still working on it. 

  The person below me is musically talented. 



  **Third Poster:** True. I play guitar in a rock band 



Source: [http://www.afterellen.com/node/43363?page=119](http://www.afterellen.com/node/43363?page=119)

### Post by tjustleft on 2009-06-03
True :)

I am glad to see the subject of DHMO being brought up here. There needs to be as much public awareness about this molecule as possible.

I myself have been a victim of DHMO and in a place one would hardly expect it. It was at the community swimming pool of all places. There I was taking swimming lessons. It should have been a happy day. 

The city must have hired someone off the streets to care for the pool. It had been filled with copious amounts of DHMO. Being odorless and colorless it was not even noticed. Can you imagine? A large group of innocent children actually swimming around in the stuff.

Being a student swimmer I had not yet perfected the Hold Your Breath Before Diving technique. It happened so fast. I went below the surface and the next thing I new I was spewing DHMO from my nose. I nearly drowned because of it. I was pulled from the pool but it was too late. I had already been affected. I had a headache and my eyes burned. They tried to blame the burning on the chlorine but we new better.

Well that's all I can say at this time. To this day it is still hard to talk about. Beware DHMO.

The person below me is going to think the person above him or her has mental issues.

That you held in your teeth, love
With the thorns underneath, love
Sticking into your gums

Your eyes cast a spell that bewitches
The last time I needed twenty stitches
To sew up the gash that you made with your lash
As we danced to the masochism tango

Bash in my brain and make me scream with pain
Then kick me once again and say we'll never part
I know too well I'm underneath your spell
So, Darling, if you smell something burning, it's my heart
Hic! Excuse me

Take your cigarette from its holder
And burn your initials in my shoulder
Fracture my spine and swear that you're mine
As we dance to the maso-chism tango


TPBM also enjoys Tom Lehrer.

### Post by HousieMousie2 on 2009-06-09

xorg.conf
```
Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Generic LCD Display"
    ModelName      "LCD Panel 1440x900"
    HorizSync       31.5 - 56.0
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 65.0
    Gamma           1
    ModeLine       "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1280x768@60" 80.14 1280 1344 1480 1680 768 769 772 795 -hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1280x720@60" 74.48 1280 1336 1472 1664 720 721 724 746 -hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1280x800@60" 83.46 1280 1344 1480 1680 800 801 804 828 -hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1440x900@60" 106.47 1440 1520 1672 1904 900 901 904 932 -hsync +vsync
EndSection
```

### Post by Marvin666 on 2009-12-21
Lets see...
The codes are commonly used in american telcom cables up to a few thousand pairs. The first 25 pairs are coded with this color code. Bundles of 25 pairs are wrapped with a ribbon marked with this code. Bundles of 600 pairs are then wrapped with a ribbon for the first color of each pair. The 25 pair cables are usually terminated to a RJ-21 connector, punch block, or patch panel. The first 4 pairs are used in cat 5e/6 cables, and some 4 pair telephone cables. Some 3 and 2 pair cables use this code as well.
I think that about sums it up.
The person below me knows something not on this list.

### Post by JayRobert on 2010-05-01
False.  The last one I watched was the last one I'll ever watch - it took about an hour too long to end.  Having drunk a large Coke, I finally gave up and went to the restroom, in fact, thinking surely it would be over by the time I got back, and yet had to sit through another half hour of scene after dull scene as every single silly narrative thread that had been opened in the previous two movies (even those opened only by inference concerning tertiary characters who were flatter than anything in Dickens), plus some open-ended narratives from various Monty Python movies, were closed in only-too-predictable fashion.  IMHO, Tolkein should have stuck to his much more interesting books on English linguistics.

(I'll admit that it wasn't as bad as Keanu Reeves in *The Day the Earth Stood Still* - but then, ***absolutely nothing*** is that bad.  I'd rather let Bill Gates hold my hand while watching the entire Lord of the Rings trilogy than sit through another Keanu Reeves movie.)

(@jetsam: BTW, thought the Becket thing was funny.)

TPBM, OTOH, *loves* The Lord of the Rings.  (And hates me.)

### Post by robsoles on 2012-01-05
Falsish more than truish: I've been dicking with mechanical and electronic devices since I was seven and am now forty-one. I gelled with programming in general quickly as a thirteen year old and I am simply pleased when this crap is ready for me to manipulate it now and won't (very much) interfere with it becoming ready till it has taken roughly double it's usually preparation time. If it manages 1.333* (twice in close memory) its usual start-up I will investigate potential claggers for a commensurate period of time (longer it takes to be ready, longer I spend looking for reasons why) but regardless findings when the time I would give it is up I get on with why I made this equipment start up in the first place.

More true in terms that if I bothered with more than simple enough maintenance (or just leaving the ruddy thing running :p :lol:) then I at least prefer the terminology "preheated start" to any ideas of "rapid" or "instant" start - better of course if it is simply the nature (basic feature) of the methodology employed that the equipment is ready sooner.


TPBM would prefer to spend up to $500 more in parts than take a (an awfully) similar system, to the one they intend to build (whoever, self, friend, customer), off the shelf.

### Post by davethebrave on 2013-02-05
I'll quote the British writer M. John Harrison and you can figure out if it's true or false from there. 

> The hero returns from her journey a wiser but humbler person, only to find that her start point has changed. It’s a rusted-out cultural madhouse, with none of the core values that demanded she go on the journey in the first place. No one remembers her or her family, they’re all different people with different ideas. There’s nothing left in the way of recognisable social situations in which to demonstrate her valuable new self; which she now sees isn’t new anyway, only out of date. She sees that she was conned all along, as much by ideas of “return” as by the idea of a goal: the journey, like the self, is both meaningless and unavoidable, but more important it is endless. The journey is without telos and what happens on the journey is the mechanics of the journey. Anything else is a wish fulfilment of the 1940s or, to be more precise, of Joseph insanely boring bloody Campbell.

TPBM has never eaten casu marzu.
